terms.definition: Creating additional training views from existing labeled examples via label-preserving transforms to increase diversity and reduce overfitting. Canonical definition in this topic.
A perturbation (flip, crop, paraphrase, noise) that changes inputs but should not change the supervised target for the task.
Applying random transforms when each training batch is loaded, so every epoch sees new variants without storing all copies.
Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique — creates synthetic minority-class tabular rows by interpolating between feature-space neighbors; mentioned here for imbalance, not full theory.
Model behavior that stays stable under transformations the task should ignore (e.g., horizontal flip for object class).
The diversity of training views the optimizer sees — augmentation increases effective size without new labels.
